campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary local rule and moving forward with plans to build a bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including applying for grant money to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth scholarship fund from Water Tower Kitchen
Extending temporary ordinance #2326
Planning a new bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Applying for state grant funding for the Hamilton Avenue bridge project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council decided which local organizations and events would receive grant money from the city's funding pool. They also approved how much money would be available for competitive grants to community events in the upcoming fiscal year, and made some clarifications to the rules for groups that want city support for events or street closures.
keyTopics: Approved specific grant recipients and dollar amounts
Set funding budget for competitive event grants for the fiscal year
Clarified rules for Community Event and Street Closure Grants

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on April 1, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the meeting through multiple platforms including cable TV, the city website, YouTube, phone, or Zoom. The meeting offered live translations in over 50 languages for residents who need them.
keyTopics: Multiple ways to watch the meeting (Cable 26, YouTube, city website, phone)
Live translations available in 50+ languages
Zoom and phone call-in options provided
No public comment period mentioned for this session

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026, where they approved previous meeting minutes and opened the floor for public comments and questions. The council also reviewed committee reports and discussed upcoming meeting schedules for various city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period
Committee and staff updates
Scheduling for boards and commissions meetings
Documents available at City Clerk's Office (500 Castro Street)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that funds housing and community development projects. The commission approved meeting minutes from the previous month and heard presentations from organizations applying for these grants to help local residents.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding for housing and community projects
Review of grant applications from community organizations
Approval of previous meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed. They discussed ways to reach out to shops at local shopping centers, including a program to help businesses catch up on licenses without penalties, and plans to encourage residents to shop locally through summer events and special promotions.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses get caught up on licenses
Outreach to shopping center businesses
Summer 'Shop Local' campaign to boost local retailers
City sponsorships partnering with private businesses
Financial incentives for small businesses, retail shops, and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 1, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Audit Committee held a meeting to review a report from the City Auditor's Office about how Silicon Valley Power (the city's power company) handles its agreements to buy electricity. The meeting was available both in-person and online for residents who wanted to watch or participate.
keyTopics: Review of Silicon Valley Power's electricity purchase agreements
City Auditor's Office findings and recommendations
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public participation options for residents

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to recognize Women's History Month and approve two main items: a disaster relief agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector for email security software (Mimecast) costing about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda items added to meeting
